description: Unique identifier for each certification record.
The kind of supplier / material certification on file — e.g. Certificate of Conformance, First Article Inspection, PPAP, FAA 8130-3, or Material Test Report.
Timestamp for when the certification record was created.
The expiry date for the certification.
The issued date for the certification.
Links each certification to its related part.
Links each certification to its related receipt.
Whether a certification is currently valid: active, expired, revoked, or pending renewal.
Links each certification to its related supplier.
Timestamp for when the customer order record was created.
Human-readable name of the customer.
The order date for the customer order.
Unique identifier for each customer order record.
Links each customer order to its related site.
Where a customer order sits in its lifecycle: entered, released, in process, shipped, invoiced, or closed. At 'shipped' the order is recognized as revenue.
Timestamp for when the customer order line record was created.
The date to start work so the item is ready by its need-by date (need-by date minus the item's lead time).
Numeric line no. recorded for each customer order line.
The date the customer needs this line's item delivered.
Links each customer order line to its related order.
Unique identifier for each customer order line record.
How many units the customer ordered on this line. Drives the production batch size; the per-unit engineering standard is multiplied by this quantity.
Links each customer order line to its related part.
Links each customer order line to its related site.
Numeric unit price recorded for each customer order line.
Timestamp for when the inv trans dist record was created.
Unique identifier for each inv trans dist record.
Numeric dist quantity recorded for each inv trans dist.
Links each inv trans dist to its related in trans.
Links each inv trans dist to its related out trans.
The class of each inventory transaction; one of a small set of values such as: R, I, A.
Timestamp for when the inventory transaction record was created.
Links each inventory transaction to its related part.
Links each inventory transaction to its related po.
Numeric quantity recorded for each inventory transaction.
The stockroom or plant site where this inventory move happened. Inventory is traced at the move, so this is where the material physically went.
The trans date for the inventory transaction.
Unique identifier for each inventory transaction record.
Direction of the stock movement: a receipt into stock (in) versus an issue out to a work order or shipment (out).
Links each inventory transaction to its related wo.
Numeric amount dollars recorded for each invoice header.
Timestamp for when the invoice header record was created.
The due date for the invoice header.
The invoice date for the invoice header.

The invoice number for each invoice header record.
The payment date for the invoice header.
Links each invoice header to its related po.
The status of each invoice header; one of a small set of values such as: Disputed, Approved, Paid, Open.
Links each invoice header to its related supplier.
Result of matching a supplier invoice against its purchase order and receiving record before it can be paid: matched, on hold for price / quantity variance, or unmatched.
Numeric burden cost recorded for each labor ticket.
The clock in for each labor ticket record.
The clock out for each labor ticket record.

The issue date for the material issue.

The issued by of each material issue; one of a small set of values such as: EMP-009, EMP-014, EMP-003, EMP-004, EMP-013, EMP-010.
Free-text part description for the material issue.

The sched start date for the operation.

Yes/no flag indicating whether the part active.
The cage code of each part; one of a small set of values such as: 59012, 1HHH9, K0579, 32499, F3234, CAGE1.
Timestamp for when the part record was created.
The drawing number of each part; one of a small set of values such as: DWG-10010-A, DWG-10011-A, DWG-10012-A, DWG-10013-A, DWG-10014-A, DWG-10024-A.
Numeric lead time days recorded for each part.
The material spec of each part; one of a small set of values such as: AMS 4928, AMS 2770, ASTM A240, BMS 8-276, AMS 5663, MS35337.
Numeric on hand quantity recorded for each part.
How a part is sourced: manufactured in-house, purchased, raw material, hardware, or outside service. Reflects engineering's make-vs-buy intent.
Free-text part description for the part.
Unique identifier for each part record.
Numeric reorder point recorded for each part.

Links each po line to its related po.
Numeric quantity recorded for each po line.
Numeric unit cost recorded for each po line.

The po date for the purchase order.

The po type of each purchase order; one of a small set of values such as: material, outside_service.
The required date for the purchase order.
Links each purchase order to its related service.
Links each purchase order to its related site.
Lifecycle stage of a purchase order: open, approved, partially or fully received, or closed.

Whether a resource requirement belongs to a PART (the as-designed routing standard — engineering, costed per unit) or a WORK_ORDER (as-built actuals — manufacturing, costed per batch). This is the engineering-vs-manufacturing differentiator.

Numeric extended cost recorded for each requirement.
Links each requirement to its related material part.
Numeric operation rowid recorded for each requirement.
Numeric operation seq recorded for each requirement.

The requirement level of each requirement; one of a small set of values such as: WORK_ORDER.
The requirement type of each requirement; one of a small set of values such as: MATERIAL.

Numeric unit cost recorded for each requirement.

The category of each suppliers; one of a small set of values such as: Raw Material, Sub-Assembly, MRO, Outside Service, material, hardware.

Numeric act bur cost recorded for each work order.
Numeric act lab cost recorded for each work order.
Numeric act mat cost recorded for each work order.
Numeric act ser cost recorded for each work order.
The close date for the work order.
Timestamp for when the work order record was created.
The date the planner wants the job released to the floor — on or before its first step begins — so material and capacity are lined up when work starts.
The open date for the work order.
Yes/no flag indicating whether the work order outside service.
Free-text part description for the work order.
Links each work order to its related part.
Number of units the work order releases to the floor (the lot / batch size). One unit reflects the engineering per-unit standard; greater than one is a manufacturing batch.
The required date for the work order.
The routing template of each work order; one of a small set of values such as: COMPOSITE, BRACKET, AIRFRAME, FASTENER, WELDMENT, MACHINED.
When the job is planned to be finished: the finish of its last routing step. Read from the operation schedule, so it reflects the planned completion of the whole job.
When the job is planned to begin: the start of its earliest routing step. Read from the operation schedule, so it reflects when the first step is set to begin on the floor.
The date the outside-service step for this work order is due.
Links each work order to its related site.
Shop-floor lifecycle stage of the work order: open, released, in process, complete, or closed.
The outside-service vendor that performs work for this work order.
Unique identifier for each work order record.
The workorder type of each work order; one of a small set of values such as: M, W.
